perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms will have the meanings specified below: “Affiliate” means, with respect to party, any individual, Partner, corporation, partnership or other entity, directly or indirectly, controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such party.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms will have the meanings specified below: “Affiliate” means, with respect to party, any individual, Partner, corporation, partnership or other entity, directly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such party. Control requires a minimum equity stake of 50%.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Affiliate' included entities 'directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control.' The modified definition adds 'Control requires a minimum equity stake of 50%.' which adds a specific constraint. In Section 4, this contradicts the text because section 4 says Each of ADP and the Partner will be responsible for compliance by its respective Affiliates with the terms of this Agreement. If an affiliate controls a company indirectly, but doens't have 50% equity, it won't be a compliant affiliate due to the 50% equity stake definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of ADP and the Partner will be responsible for compliance by its respective Affiliates with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Affiliate' allowed for indirect control. The changed definition adds the requirement of a minimum 50% equity stake for control. The contradicted text states that each party is responsible for its Affiliates' compliance. Because the change specifies the 50% minimum equity stake, an entity that was originally considered an affiliate due to indirect control, but which doesn't meet the 50% equity stake, will no longer be considered an affiliate. This leads to a contradiction, since the party is not responsible for ensuring compliance by the entity with indirect control that does not meet the equity threshold.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be used primarily for the purpose of Partner’s submission of its Application for evaluation by ADP for the potential sale of, promotion of, access to and/or marketing of, such Application in the ADP Marketplace to Prospects through the ADP Marketplace, and may also be used for internal training purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of permitted use was 'solely for the purpose of Partner’s submission...and for no other purpose.' The modified version adds 'primarily for the purpose...' and 'may also be used for internal training purposes.' This creates ambiguity. This creates an internal contradiction with Section 3, because Section 3 states, 'Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above)...', the internal training is an additional use case, conflicting with this statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion. The Receiving Party will be responsible for the compliance of its Representatives with the terms of this Agreement. If a Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement and the Receiving Party will request that all Confidential Information so disclosed is treated confidentially (and any such disclosure by the Receiving Party will not violate the terms of this Agreement). The Disclosing Party shall reimburse the Receiving Party for all of its reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the terms of the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the use of Confidential Information 'solely' for a specific purpose. The changed text broadens this to 'primarily' for that purpose and allows for 'internal training purposes.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Receiving Party will 'not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above).' The introduction of 'internal training purposes' as an acceptable use directly conflicts with the exclusivity outlined in the contradicted text, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential using reasonable care and discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says the receiving party must keep the information confidential by using the same care and discretion as their own, which is no less than reasonable care. The modified version removes the 'no less than reasonable care' threshold. This creates ambiguity. This causes an internal contradiction with Section 7, which states: 'The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.' If the the Representative only keeps information with only 'reasonable care', this may conflict with the required to 'reasonably cooperate' and there is no way of knowing if that is their own practice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the Receiving Party's Representatives must use at least reasonable care and discretion, referencing their own standards but setting a minimum threshold. The changed text removes this minimum threshold, requiring only reasonable care and discretion. This creates a potential conflict with the contradicted text. The contradicted text requires the receiving party to 'reasonably cooperate' if there is unauthorized use, but if the Representative is only acting with 'reasonable care', it calls into question if they would 'reasonably cooperate' because there is no longer a floor of compliance that would suggest they would cooperate to the full extent that is necessary based on their own practices. This is a direct conflict according to the definition provided, as it introduces ambiguity where certainty was expected, potentially affecting the enforcement of the 'reasonably cooperate' clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for three years from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for three years from the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier by either party with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement defines that the agreement must remain for three years. The added text adds that either party can terminate it with a 30-day notice. This creates conflicting terms. This contradicts section 5: 'Except for the obligation of confidentiality and the restrictions on use imposed by this Agreement upon the Receiving Party, each party acknowledges that no obligation of any kind is assumed or implied against the other party by virtue of any meeting or any discussion regarding the Evaluation with respect to whatever information is exchanged'. The phrase no obligation of any kind could be argued to conflict with the three year term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except for the obligation of confidentiality and the restrictions on use imposed by this Agreement upon the Receiving Party, each party acknowledges that no obligation of any kind is assumed or implied against the other party by virtue of any meeting or any discussion regarding the Evaluation with respect to whatever information is exchanged. Further, this Agreement and any meetings or communications of the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement will not (i) constitute any offer, request, or contract among the parties to engage in any transaction, nor (ii) constitute any offer, request or contract involving a buyer-seller relationship, venture, teaming or partnership relationship among the parties. Each party hereto hereby acknowledges that the Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information disclosed by it.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement remains in effect for three years. The changed text allows either party to terminate with 30 days' notice, conflicting with the three-year term. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'no obligation of any kind is assumed,' as the original three-year term implies an obligation, while the modified version introduces an escape clause that undermines this obligation. Therefore, the changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will reasonably cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will use commercially reasonable efforts to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, and will cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure. Notification is required only if the breach is material.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says 'promptly' and 'any', while the modified version says 'commercially reasonable efforts' and 'material', which introduces vagueness on if the Receiving Party is complying. The modification creates an internal contradiction within Section 3, specifically relating to the instruction for Representatives to keep information confidential. The addition of a 'material' threshold now means that Representatives might inadvertently deem a leak immaterial, preventing disclosure, causing a representative to be considered as non confidential with terms in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, the Receiving Party shall treat as confidential and will not use (other than for the purpose of evaluating the Partner Application as stated in Section 2 above), disclose or otherwise make available any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any person other than Representatives of the Receiving Party who have a business need to know Mutual Non-­‐Disclosure Agreement Version 2 2 for purposes of the Evaluation, and who have been advised by the Receiving Party of the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will instruct its Representatives who have access to the Confidential Information to keep the same confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with respect to its own confidential property and trade secrets, which will be no less than reasonable care and discretion. The Receiving Party will be responsible for the compliance of its Representatives with the terms of this Agreement. If a Receiving Party is required by applicable law, regulation, court order or legal process to disclose any Confidential Information, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice of such request or requirement and the Receiving Party will request that all Confidential Information so disclosed is treated confidentially (and any such disclosure by the Receiving Party will not violate the terms of this Agreement). The Disclosing Party shall reimburse the Receiving Party for all of its reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred in connection with its compliance with the terms of the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it modifies the original obligation of 'promptly' notifying the Disclosing Party of 'any' unauthorized use or disclosure to 'commercially reasonable efforts' and only for 'material' breaches. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the Receiving Party to instruct its Representatives to keep Confidential Information confidential by using the same care and discretion that the Receiving Party uses with its own confidential property, but no less than reasonable care. The addition of 'material' threshold in the changed text means that a Representative might deem a leak immaterial, preventing disclosure, causing a representative to be considered as non-confidential with terms in Section 3 (the contradicted text), creating an internal contradiction, because the standard has changed to a subjective materiality threshold.
